findings.description: Typography rules (Agenda as primary headline font, Nunito Sans for body copy, plus the associated font-family declarations/import links) are present in the source but have no corresponding rule in the candidate set. Only the color hierarchy rule was extracted, leaving an entire typography governance area unclustered.

payload.description: Agenda must be used for all headlines/headers; Nunito Sans must be used for all body copy in print, with specified font stack/import.

findings.description: The CTA shape/treatment instruction and its accompanying SVG gradient definition are unclustered — no rule captures the required CTA shape or its color treatment, despite being an explicit directive ('use this shape and treatment for CTAs').

payload.description: CTAs must use the specified rounded-rectangle shape with the defined gradient fill.

findings.description: The CTA gradient SVG uses hex colors #FFDF55 and #FAA21B, which do not exactly match the three approved brand colors (#00529B, #FAA31B, #FFC60A) declared in the color hierarchy rule. This unflagged discrepancy suggests either a drifted/unauthorized color variant or a missed relation linking CTA treatment to the approved palette that should be reconciled or noted as an exception.

The color hierarchy rule is marked hard_constraint/must but lacks any governance facet (e.g., scope of applicability such as 'internal banners only', owner/approver, or exception process) despite being sourced from an internal-only doc_ref; no explicit imperative language ('must') appears in the source sentence itself, only a descriptive hierarchy listing.

payload.governance_note: Add explicit scope/owner metadata; verify hardness given descriptive (not imperative) source phrasing.
